text: The surgeon conducts a thorough exploration of the abdominal cavity, revealing tumor implants located on the right flank, right iliac fossa, right colon serosa, and right fallopian tube. The Peritoneal Cancer Index (PCI) score is determined to be five.
The surgeon begins the procedure with a right parietal peritonectomy, carefully dissecting the peritoneum and retroperitoneum up to the parietocolic junction.

The surgeon excises mucinous implants from the right colon serosa using extra mucosal excision. The right ovarian vessels are identified and sectioned, followed by a right salpingo-oophorectomy ensuring oncological margins. The right parietal peritonectomy is completed while preserving the right round uterine ligament.
The surgeon performs a left salpingo-oophorectomy, followed by reinforcement of the excised surface on the right colon wall using interrupted sutures.

The surgeon resects the appendicular stump using an endogia stapler. The patient's position is adjusted to an anti-Trendelenburg orientation to facilitate access to the upper abdomen, where the hepatic round ligament is removed.

The surgeon begins a complete omentectomy by opening the transcavity from the major gastric curvature, then proceeds toward the right side, carefully dissecting and preserving the gastroepiploic vessels.

The surgeon completes the right omentectomy by dissecting along the transverse colon up to the hepatic colonic flexure. The left omentectomy begins by following the major gastric curvature up to the splenic hilum, then continues along the transverse colon up to the splenic flexure, completing the total omentectomy.

Following complete cytoreduction, the surgeon employs a HIPEC CO₂ close technique device for administering hyperthermic intraperitoneal chemotherapy. Cannulas are inserted through laparoscopic ports, and mitomycin is administered for pseudomyxoma treatment.
A transparent device is inserted through the umbilical port to visually assess the level of chemotherapy during HIPEC administration, utilizing the CO₂ agitation system.
